title: The Three Categories of Pro-Lifers
author: Dusty Deevers
orientation: Dusty Deevers maps the self-described Pro-Life Movement into three internal factions—the Establishment (Big Pro-life), the Conflicted-consistent, and the Blurry Middle—then contrasts all three with biblical Abolitionism and calls Christians to #PLEXIT.
audiences: Self-identified pro-life Christian wondering which category they fit
Pastor or church leader weighing which movement to align with
Activist or legislator deciding between incremental and equal-protection bills
Skeptic or pro-choice reader trying to understand why abolitionists distinguish themselves from pro-lifers
Donor or supporter evaluating Pro-Life Establishment organizations

questions.question: What three groups does Dusty Deevers identify within the current Pro-Life Movement?
questions.audience: Self-identified pro-life Christian wondering which category they fit
questions.answer: Deevers divides Pro-Lifers into the Establishment (Big Pro-life), the Conflicted-consistent who hold the biblical view but still self-label as pro-life, and the Blurry Middle who sense the compromise but remain stuck in midwayism.
questions.quote:
1. Establishment or Big Pro-life
2. Conflicted-consistent
3. Blurry middle

questions.question: What are the four defining tenets of the Pro-Life Establishment according to their May 12, 2022 coalition letter?
questions.audience: Donor or supporter evaluating Pro-Life Establishment organizations
questions.answer: The Establishment treats women seeking abortion as second victims, refuses to support equal protection from fertilization, treats post-abortive trauma as evidence of victimhood, and commits to incrementalism that never criminalizes the act of preborn homicide.
questions.quote:
1. “Second-victim.” Women seeking preborn homicide (abortion) are always victims.
2. Deny equal protection from fertilization/conception. Never support any measure or legislation to criminalize the act of preborn homicide from fertilization for all parties to the homicide.
3. Post-abortive trauma is evidence of victimhood.
4. [Incrementalism](https://freethestates.org/immediate-not-gradual/index.html). Continue to pursue a pragmatic, incremental strategy that at no point criminalizes the act of preborn homicide.

questions.question: Why does Deevers say the Pro-Life Establishment results in implicit Christological heresy?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader weighing which movement to align with
questions.answer: By denying personhood from fertilization and refusing to criminalize preborn homicide as murder, the Establishment’s logic also denies the true personhood of Christ and refuses Him equal protection from conception.
questions.quote: 3. Results in implicit Christological heresy—not affirming the true personhood of Christ or granting Him equal protection from fertilization/conception.

questions.question: How does Deevers characterize the relationship between Big Pro-life and equal-protection legislation in the states?
questions.audience: Activist or legislator deciding between incremental and equal-protection bills
questions.answer: He says Big Pro-life actively wars against equal protection bills and has a documented record of slandering the Christians pushing them, with examples in Idaho, Indiana, Oklahoma, and Texas.
questions.quote: Big Pro-life wars against equal protection/criminalization bills in every state and actively [slanders, misaligns, and lies](https://freethestates.org/2022/09/sbc-president-slanders-sbc-pastor/index.html) about Christians holding to a consistent, biblical position.

questions.question: What does the Conflicted-consistent group affirm, and what does Deevers tell them to do about it?
questions.audience: Self-identified pro-life Christian wondering which category they fit
questions.answer: They affirm life and personhood from fertilization and the duty to grant equal protection by criminalizing preborn homicide, but Deevers calls them to stop assuming the Establishment can be reformed and to #PLEXIT into Abolitionism.
questions.quote: This group needs to #PLEXIT. Leave the Pro-Life Movement for the biblically consistent [Abortion Abolition](https://freethestates.org/abolitionism-101-resources/index.html) position.

questions.question: Why does Deevers argue the Pro-Life Establishment cannot be reformed from within?
questions.audience: Activist or legislator deciding between incremental and equal-protection bills
questions.answer: Because Big Pro-life is structurally compromised—funded by donors who want abortion exceptions and committed to secular pragmatism—so individuals may convert but the institution itself will remain biblically compromised.
questions.quote: It is to say that Big Pro-life always will be biblically compromised, well-funded by those wanting abortion exceptions and allowances, and politically secular and pragmatic.

questions.question: How does Deevers describe the Blurry Middle's posture and the recipe that keeps them stuck?
questions.audience: Self-identified pro-life Christian wondering which category they fit
questions.answer: The Blurry Middle sees Big Pro-life’s compromises and feels pulled toward consistency, but a mix of pragmatism, tradition, and Pro-life pride yields a lukewarm “midwayism” rather than abolition.
questions.quote: The third Pro-life group sees the compromises of Big Pro-life and is tugged toward a more biblically consistent position, but a pint of pragmatism, a teaspoon of tradition, and an ounce of Big Pro-life pride is a sure recipe for a bland, lukewarm loaf of midwayism.

questions.question: What does the GATES acrostic stand for in Abolitionism?
questions.audience: Skeptic or pro-choice reader trying to understand why abolitionists distinguish themselves from pro-lifers
questions.answer: GATES summarizes the five biblical tenets of Abolitionism: Gospel-centered, Aligned Providentially, Through the Church, Engaged Biblically, and Sought Immediately without Compromise.
questions.quote:
G – Gospel-centered  
A – Aligned Providentially  
T – Through the Church  
E – Engaged Biblically  
S – Sought Immediately without Compromise

questions.question: What five things must an actual abolition bill do, per Deevers?
questions.audience: Activist or legislator deciding between incremental and equal-protection bills
questions.answer: An abolition bill must outlaw abortion from fertilization, include no homicide exceptions, criminalize preborn homicide with equal protection, refuse to submit to unconstitutional rulings like Roe, and repeal or supersede statutes permitting preborn homicide.
questions.quote:
1. Outlaw abortion from fertilization/conception.
2. Do not include exceptions for preborn homicide (ectopics and emergencies are *not* preborn homicide).
3. Criminalize preborn homicide and establish justice as equal protection for the preborn.
4. Do not submit to unconstitutional rulings (like Roe).
5. Repeal or supersede all statutes which allow for preborn homicide.

questions.question: What is Deevers' closing call to action for Christians still inside the Pro-Life Movement?
questions.audience: Pastor or church leader weighing which movement to align with
questions.answer: He calls for a mass exodus of Christians from the Pro-life Movement into Abolitionism—the #PLEXIT—because abortion is murder and it is past time to act like it.
questions.quote: It’s past time for a mass exodus of Christians from the Pro-life Movement. It’s past time for a Christian Pro-life exit in favor of abolitionism. It’s time to #PLEXIT.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Reinforces Deevers' #PLEXIT thesis by making the case for dropping the Pro-Life label entirely.

related.title: Christian, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Develops the same identity argument from a confessional angle—Christians shouldn’t accept the Pro-life label.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Expands the article’s critique of Establishment incrementalism with concrete reasoning.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Provides the foundational strategic dichotomy underlying Deevers' three-category map.

related.title: Why Do Pro-Lifers Despise Abolitionists?
related.reason: Documents the hostility from Big Pro-life that Deevers describes as slander against consistent Christians.

related.title: My Pro-Life-to-Abolitionist Conversion
related.reason: Personal narrative of the very #PLEXIT journey Deevers urges the Conflicted-consistent to make.

related.title: Catechism for Anti-Abolitionists (Pro-Choice and Pro-Life)
related.reason: Sharpens the contrast between Abolitionism and both pro-choice and Establishment pro-life positions.
